response.justification: Bogdan is a skilled full-stack and systems engineer with genuine breadth, but the role's core requirement — production-grade C++ or Rust development — is effectively unmet. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis; his primary stack for the past decade has been TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. While the GISCollective project demonstrates real geospatial interest and the Sociomantic role shows low-latency systems thinking, neither translates to the routing tile pipeline engineering and navigation-system domain expertise this position demands.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS applications
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems thinking from Sociomantic's micro-services work handling millions of requests per second
Consistent track record of operating across the full technical stack and picking up new problem domains independently
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or any Rust experience — the two acceptable primary languages for this role
No routing tile generation, navigation pipeline, or guidance-system experience at any point in career
Career trajectory is firmly in web/product engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) with no signals of pivoting toward systems/native code
